note: Initial implementation passes acceptance correctness tests

results: single_tile PASS; multi_tile_W FAIL with pre-existing precision baseline issue (max abs 0.254 — matches Phase-0 baseline exactly, not introduced by refinement)
note: shape (1,1,32,256) dim=-1 → total_lanes=1 → runs on a single core, so precision is identical to pre-refinement. Confirms RT-arg plumbing works for the single-core degenerate case. Need to test shapes with total_lanes > 1 to actually exercise multi-core distribution.

Bit-identical to Phase 0 baseline (per changelog: 17/26 acceptance pass; 9 failures all the pre-existing atol=0.01 precision-floor issue documented for Refinement 4). Multi-core refactor introduced NO regression on shapes that already passed, and did NOT improve the 9 pre-existing precision failures (precision is unaffected by core count — same numeric path).

Multi-core distribution validated: 1-lane single-core fallback, 16-lane partial grid, 64-lane exact-fill, 65/96/130-lane remainder distributions, 33-lane multi-block-per-lane decomposition, determinism (bit-equal across runs). Both dim=-1 and dim=-2.

Multi-core refactor preserves the Phase 0 results bit-identically: 8/8 precision_baseline (PCC >= 0.999), 9/9 extended (PCC + rel_rms checks, determinism, memory_config kwarg). Includes shape (2,4,64,128) which under multi-core distributes 8 lanes/16 lanes across 8/16 cores.

Production-mode run confirms no race conditions across multi-core distribution. The kernels behave correctly under realistic dispatch timing on 1, 16, 64, 65, 96, 130 lane regimes for both dim=-1 and dim=-2. Refinement 1 complete.
First smoke test after refactoring program descriptor + reader + compute kernels to support shape-aware input buffering strategy (Refinement 2). Single-tile shape lands on WHOLE_ROW_DB strategy.

NO REGRESSION vs Phase 0 — same 17/26 pass rate as the changelog Phase-0 baseline. All 9 failures are the pre-existing atol=0.01 precision-floor issue on shapes with reduce_dim_tiles >= 2 (Refinement 4 scope), now also observable on dim=-2 multi-tile shapes which use the new WHOLE_ROW_DB strategy. All dim=-1 tests pass — the path through the matmul-based REDUCE_ROW remains numerically identical to Phase 0 because the bulk reduce<> on (1, Wt) decomposes into the same per-tile SrcA TF32 dot products as the per-block accumulate_reduce.

All 8 precision-baseline cases pass (4 shapes x 2 dims). The new WHOLE_ROW strategy preserves Phase-0 PCC and abs/RMS thresholds — per-lane numerics unchanged (same mul → reduce → sub → mul sequence; only CB layout and traversal order differ).
Extended (9/9) + Multicore (18/18) all pass — no regression in either suite. Multicore distribution + determinism behave identically because per-lane numeric path is unchanged.

Confirmed strategy boundaries: Wt<=28 -> WHOLE_ROW_DB (e.g. W=32/256/896); 29<=Wt<=42 -> WHOLE_ROW_SB (W=1024/1344); Wt>=43 -> PER_TILE_STREAM (W=1376/2048). Mirror boundaries hold for dim=-2 on Ht. These will inform the test parametrization.
